summary: Window's (and derived nodes) background panels do not scale with title_hight theme property

description.text: ### Tested versions Tested in 4.4 stable, 4.5 beta 3 ### System information Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - integrated Intel(R) Arc(TM) Graphics (Intel Corporation; 32.0.101.6874) - Intel(R) Core(TM) Ultra 5 125H (18 threads) ### Issue description When creating a custom theme, a window's panel does not grow when the title_height property is changed. However, the bounding box of the window IS affected, as the mouse indicated the region where you can grab the border has moved. As there are no options for a title panel or title panel size, I assume the panel is supposed to grow but is not doing so. In this image, a theme with larger icons and font size runs off the panel but remains in-bounds: The effect can clearly be seen when setting the title_height property of FileDialog (or window) to a large value (e.g., 150) and seeing the mouse changes to the scaling icon well above the top of the bounding box. I wasn't able to capture a screenshot with my cursor visible; please see the MRP This is a problem, since when creating a theme with larger text and/or icons, I have no way to increase the bounds to encompass it. ### Steps to reproduce Create a new theme For Window (or a child), attach a theme and set the title_height property to some value larger than the default (36) Run the scene and observe that the bounding box of the window grows upwards, but not the panel. Alternatively, see the MRP attached. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [windowbug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21824561/windowbug.zip)

summary: Built-in or external editor's go-to-definition fails to jump to source for autoloads that appear in type annotations

description.text:
### Tested versions - Reproducible in: - v4.5.beta.custom_build [0c51ede24] (compiled from source on 2025-08-17 without any changes), - v4.5.beta5.official [c81fd6c51], - v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6], and - v4.0.stable.official [92bee43ad]. ### System information Godot v4.5.beta5 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 5 2600X Six-Core Processor (12 threads) - 15.95 GiB memory ### Issue description Preface: both the built-in Godot code editor as well as e.g. VS Code (with [`godot-vscode-plugin`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-vscode-plugin)) generally support jumping to the definition of a symbol. This functionality is usually bound to `Ctrl + Left Click`, as is the case here (at least by default). Issue: in specific cases, neither the built-in Godot code editor nor an external language server client (e.g. [`godot-vscode-plugin`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-vscode-plugin)) are able to jump-to-definition of an _autoload_ script. However, this happens _only if the autoload type appears in the code as a type annotation_ (this includes function parameter types and return type annotations), not an identifier. I've looked around the bug tracker, and there were some slightly similar issues with autoloads (#100680, #73058), but I believe none of these cover this problem. --- #### Example So, again, jumping to the definition of an autoload does work in e.g. function bodies or at the top level when the symbol is an identifier, but _doesn't_ when it is in the position of a type annotation. Here's an example: **I'll provide a complete MRP below,** but here's the gist of the problem in code snippets: ```gdscript # [file: auto_load_foo.gd] extends Node static func static_foo() -> void: pass func foo() -> void: pass ``` Let's suppose that this `auto_load_foo.gd` is now registered as an autoload named `AutoLoadFoo`. It therefore appears in auto-completion (and is generally available) elsewhere. Let's now suppose that we create a new script named `some_script.gd` and attach it to some node in a scene: ```gdscript # [file: some_script.gd] # ... # This type lookup doesn't work. var some_foo: AutoLoadFoo = null func some_function( # This type lookup doesn't work either. auto_load_foo_param: AutoLoadFoo ) -> void: # But this does. AutoLoadFoo.static_foo() auto_load_foo_param.foo() ```
**The bug:** Ctrl-clicking on the `AutoLoadFoo` type on the variable type annotation *or* in the function parameter list, you'd expect to jump to `auto_load_foo.gd`, but *nothing happens*. However, if you ctrl-click on any of the things in the `some_function` body, the jump succeeds. So supposedly something is wrong specifically in the logic for type annotations (see below for more info).
--- #### Further details and cause So here's how I went about figuring this out (sorry, there's a lot of text). You don't have to read this one I guess, it's more or less my thought process.
How I found the solution (expandable) I initially observed this in VS Code with [`godot-vscode-plugin` (`godot-tools`)](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-vscode-plugin), so I spun up a local extension to see what the Godot language server was sending back. Turns out, when requesting the definition of an autoload specifically, the language server would return an empty array. This was not the case for other types, such as other class names of scripts that were used elsewhere in other scenes. This led me to believe that the problem was not in the extension, but in the language server (or underlying scripting engine or whatever - as you'll see, I'm not familiar with the engine internals - forgive me, this is my first time poking at them). I confirmed this by checking `Ctrl + Left Click` in the built-in Godot editor. The same problem occurred: the editor would normally jump to the definition, but not if the thing I was ctrl-clicking on was an autoload in the type annotation position. This includes function parameter types and function return types, which I suppose makes sense, they are type annotations after all. I then cloned the Godot repository and built the engine from the latest commit on `master`. Same problem. I chased the bug around by breakpointing the [`GDScriptTextDocument::definition`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/0c51ede2434d99d2c80dc87b7df179db66537f1c/modules/gdscript/language_server/gdscript_text_document.cpp#L415-L421) language server method, and then finally observed the following.
When looking for the definition of an autoload, the editor would call [`GDScriptWorkspace::resolve_symbol`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/0c51ede2434d99d2c80dc87b7df179db66537f1c/modules/gdscript/language_server/gdscript_workspace.cpp#L770). This would succeed for global classes, because the [`ScriptServer::is_global_class` call](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/0c51ede2434d99d2c80dc87b7df179db66537f1c/modules/gdscript/language_server/gdscript_workspace.cpp#L789) would return `true` for them, but not for autoloads. *I have no knowledge of Godot internals, so it's possible the bug is here already, not where I fixed it?* Regardless. For autoloads, I eventually arrived at [`GDScriptLanguage::lookup_code`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/0c51ede2434d99d2c80dc87b7df179db66537f1c/modules/gdscript/gdscript_editor.cpp#L4295), which is called if `is_global_class` returns `false`. All requests for definitions where an autoload is involved seem to reach this point, even if those autoloads appear in the code as an identifier, not a type. This is where I applied the fix: the reason normal autoload lookups (where the symbol was e.g. an identifier, not a type) would succeed was found in [this switch statement](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/0c51ede2434d99d2c80dc87b7df179db66537f1c/modules/gdscript/gdscript_editor.cpp#L4392). Identifiers would execute [this branch](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/0c51ede2434d99d2c80dc87b7df179db66537f1c/modules/gdscript/gdscript_editor.cpp#L4416-L4556), while type annotations would execute [just this](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/0c51ede2434d99d2c80dc87b7df179db66537f1c/modules/gdscript/gdscript_editor.cpp#L4614-L4619). Notice how the first branch would [explicitly check for autoloads](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/0c51ede2434d99d2c80dc87b7df179db66537f1c/modules/gdscript/gdscript_editor.cpp#L4483-L4501), but the second wouldn't. I basically just added that same code segment into the second branch, as the lookup (for autoloads specifically) seemed to be missing, and autoload types certainly can appear in type annotations.
I chased this bug around for so long that I might as well send a PR - ~~I'll do so shortly~~ ~~I'll do so if [my proposed solution](https://github.com/simongoricar/godot/commit/4016ec0ebcf89f48b85c4e6c01b4056bedcee642) seems sane.~~ Done. --- ### Steps to reproduce See the MRP (and example in the issue description). The setup in the MRP is as follows: - `autoloaded_script.gd` defines a simple class, which is then defined as an autoload under the name `AutoLoadedFoo`. - As expected, the symbol `AutoLoadedFoo` appears in auto-completion project-wide. - `some_scene_script.gd` defines `SomeSceneScript`, an empty class we'll need later. This script is attached to `SomeScene.tscn`. - As expected, the symbol `SomeSceneScript` appears in auto-completion project-wide. Now take a look at `consumer_script.gd`: notice how "Go to definition" works for `AutoLoadedFoo` only in cases of the top-level or function body, **but only when the autoload appears as e.g. an identifier, never when used as a type annotation**. `SomeSceneScript` doesn't exhibit the same problems. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) The following is the minimal reproduction project: [godot-autoload-definition-jump-bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21824011/godot-autoload-definition-jump-bug.zip).

description.text:
Update: The newest driver 581.42 fixes this issue.
https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/drivers/details/257133/

### Tested versions

Reproducible: in all stable releases of 4.x. (tested)
Not present in: 3.4.1 or 3.6.1. (tested)

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - 16B RAM -Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8088) driver 580.88 - D27-30 @ 60hz -AMD Ryzen 5 3600 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

Issue Effects: Editor Camera and Game Camera
There seems to be a regression in Godot 4.x that causes visual jittering that was not present in Godot 3.x. This effects 'both' the in editor camera 'and' game camera, so I assume it's related.

V-Sync: (enabled) and this still occurs.
(Note: If v-sync is off; this issue doesn't happen.)

* However; the issue itself is inconsistent in 4.x as it can go from smooth to jittering with v-sync on.
(3.x is not affected)


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b064db8e-4047-4bf7-b7e6-70416a1b0e67

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4f22c42e-29ab-4480-9ec8-c664b4cc865b

Godot 3.x stable expected behavior

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/65fd0e17-33ca-4a3d-a486-d91c143302ea







### Steps to reproduce

Reproduction:

1. Open either project (EditorOnly or GodotGame)

2. Move the camera around an object while looking at it.

3. If the issue doesn’t appear immediately:

	* Try alt-tabbing or changing window focus.

	* Be persistent the jitter will eventually show.

4. Note: The issue may be masked by certain frame smoothing or frame generation features. (untested)

N/A but it's more noticeable with visible nodes.

GodotGame: The playable environment to help find the issue in the player's camera view.

summary: Improve and reduce Game window sizing

description.text: **Editor window minimum size, before:** **Editor window minimum size, after:** Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12060 1) Now the top bar is `FlowContainer` which means that there will be no issues with width. 2) All embedding options were moved to single submenu and its icon is changed to see the difference between, for example, camera override options. 3) I decided to move `Toggle Selection Visibility` button to the selection buttons section, since they are directly related. 4) `Panel` where game window is placed on run is replaced with `PanelContainer` (there were sizing issues with it and the label inside it). 5) There is a problem that the Floating Game Workspace does not increase the size of the window to the right, as it was before: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b9ad534d-ae98-4ea6-b812-85a1fe0b2dfa If this is not ok, feel free to suggest how it can be fixed (same is valid for another changes I made).

summary: Custom properties of a C# Tool class reset when .NET project built while scene is open

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - macOS Sequoia (15.6.0) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon Pro 5300M - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-9750H CPU @ 2.60GHz (12 threads)

### Issue description

Custom properties (defined with overridden `_Get`/`_Set`/`_GetPropertyList`) on a C# Tool class (required to edit custom properties in the editor) can have their values reset in the scene, if the .NET project is built _while the scene is open_.

If the .NET project is built from the editor while the scene is open, only custom properties backed directly by a top level field in the class will retain their customized values.
Custom properties whose values are really stored inside some sub-object will be reset.

All custom properties are populated correctly when a scene is first opened, as when loading the scene Godot calls the custom `_Set` implementation for such properties (after calling the constructor), passing `_Set` the persisted property values from the scene file. This seems like expected behaviour here, and is already working correctly when the scene is opened from disk.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open sample project
2. **Close** the default scene
3. Click _**Build Project**_ button
4. Open `test3d.tscn`
5. Node `Test3d/MyThingyCs` has custom properties:
    * `MyName` - stored directly in public string field on `MyThingy`
    * `MyBuggyName` - stored inside a private field `MyThingyCs._myBuggyNameData.MyBuggyName` - `MyThingy._myBuggyNameData` value is constructed in the `MyThingyCs` constructor
6. `MyName` and `MyBuggyName` are both currently set to "Graham" (from `.tscn` file).
Custom `_Set` method is only called to set `MyBuggyName` (see console log), `MyName` property is somehow set in some other way.
7. Make some minor edit to `src/MyThingy.cs` (eg. add extra newline) so that it must be rebuilt
8. While `test3d.tscn` is still open, click _**Build Project**_ button
9. Note that `MyBuggyName` property is now reset to default value "Bob"

I tested same behaviour for custom properties of a GDScript file, stored in an underlying .NET object (see `my_thingy_gd.gd` that stores it's custom properties in `MyThingyCs.cs`) and it has the same effect.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[godotcscustomsetter_2025-08-17_21-04-40.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21823335/godotcscustomsetter_2025-08-17_21-04-40.zip)

summary: Transparent Window doesn't work correctly on Windows, also ColorPicker pick screen color

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 4.5 beta 5
- Reproducible in Godot 4.4

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot v4.5.beta5 - Vulkan (Foward+) - Dedicated NVIDEA GeForce 1070

### Issue description

Transparent windows behaves very strangely on Windows 10*.
This might be a very specific bug so I was wondering if anyone else has seen this behavior?

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1cf26e9f-e7a3-4fa8-b095-58da5ed0d020

If I turn on **borderless**, 
it works properly and displays the screen behind correctly, so its not that big of an issue.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ad32d39d-0397-48bb-8d31-312bc98e5006

Another problem that might be related to this is that my **ColorPicker** 'pick screen color' also doesn't work.

It always shows the black color on the floating color preview.

But sometimes when I move the mouse fast, I can see the colors for a split second.

_(Also, for some reason the white borders around the color rect does not show in any recording software I tried, so its only a black square in the video. Tried with ScreenToGif, ShareX and OBS screen recording)_

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/410ee6ff-822e-411c-afe2-47709bdee6ad

The ColorPicker works normally if I enable **Single Window Mode** for the Editor. 

Which is why I think the problem is related to the floating (possibly transparent) window of the color pick preview.



-----
I'm almost positive this is some incompatibility with older window stuff since I've never seen anyone with this problem. 
So I don't have high hopes this can be fixed, but just in case this is something else I'll leave this issue open.

**And for some context**: I'm running Windows 10 1803 (17134.1246). I ran out of SSD to update my windows years ago. 

So I can't even run games that says it "Requires Windows 10" because what it actually means it that it requires windows 10 1903. 
But since Godot still supports windows 8 that should not have been a problem.

Also my nvidea drivers are stuck at 531.41 from 2023. When I updated them this year it kept giving me BSOD to I had to go back.
I also had a few programs refuse to run things saying it my CPU lacks "SSE4.2 instruction set", even tho my i7-7700 technically has it.

Which is why I said this is likely something VERY specific, of maybe even a miss-configuration of something I did years ago.

Sorry for the long text, feel free to close this if no one else finds this problem.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable the Project settings:
display/window/size/transparent
display/window/per_pixel_transparency/allowed
rendering/viewport/transparent_background

2. Make sure embed window is disabled
3. Start the game

**Result**: Transparent background is not transparent, makes background glitchy
**Expected**: Transparent background.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Any empty project should do it. But just in case here's the one I used. I left the '.godot' folder just in case too.

[transparent_project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21821988/transparent_project.zip)

summary: Stutters/Strange Frame Instability using Forward+/Vulkan with Vsync.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested in 4.5 beta 5. 
Found initially in 4.4.1


### System information

Windows 11 -  Vulkan / Forward+ - Nvidia RTX 3070 (driver update 580.97) - Intel i7 12700f

### Issue description

Using the forward+ renderer with VSync (either regular or GSync), while the games max fps is set to the refresh rate causes very strange stuttering issues. What will happen is that the scene will run fine but usually after a few seconds the scene will start stuttering for seconds at a time. During this time, the process delta will vary by multiple milliseconds, causing stutters. It will also make mouse input movements, such as camera control, feel like they operate at half of the desired refresh rate, whereas the moving mesh in the background seems to operate more in line with the proper refresh rate.
What triggers the stuttering is hard to tell. It usually will start acting up without any input after a few seconds. Alt tabbing in and out of the window a few times also seems to cause the stutters.

Stutters do not appear to happen when VSync is off.
Stutters do not appear to happen when D3D12 is used instead of Vulkan.
Stutters do not appear to happen if the scene operates at less than the monitors refresh rate.
Stutters can happen with Windowed, Fullscreen and Exclusive Fullscreen.
Turning off GSync, both on the monitor itself and in Nvidia control panel does not cause the stuttering to go away.
Messing with presentation mode in the Nvidia control panel does not seem to affect anything.
Increasing the physics tick rate of the project seems to worsen the delta changing. Likewise, lessening it seems to lower it.

Windows Dynamic Refresh Rate setting is not enabled, and also not available on my machine.

Here are videos of the stutters triggered by both alt tabbing and waiting. In the alt tabbing video I move the camera around with a mouse.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ccf0c363-2b84-4f44-b5b8-b42d104e7ae9

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/21b0362e-5b57-4d5b-af88-fff13b6993cb

Initially thought to be #84137 , however, I am not getting Jitters, only stutters, and upon further testing, I got it the stuttering to appear with Windowed mode, which was said to not cause the issue there.
Potentially similar to #90733 , however, I've been able to trigger the stutters with different conditions listed.


### Steps to reproduce

summary: [MacOS] Godot will launch in a desktop containing a Finder window instead of the current desktop

description.text:
### Tested versions

* Reproducible in 4.5-dev2 and later (up to 4.5-beta5).
* Not reproducible in 4.4.1 and 4.5-dev1.

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta5 - macOS Sequoia (15.6.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 Pro (Apple8) - Apple M2 Pro (10 threads) - 16.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

Since 4.5-dev2, Godot will not launch in the current MacOS active desktop if any other desktop includes an open Finder window: the active desktop will automatically change to the one containing the last open Finder window and Godot's project manager window will launch behind that Finder window.

If multiple desktops include a Finder window, the last one to have been opened is targeted.

### Steps to reproduce

This screen recording shows the steps used to reproduce the behavior consistently:

* Initial desktop is 1 (desktop number is displayed as a number in the system's top right menubar for convenience), no Finder window is open.
* Moving to desktop 2 and launching Godot results in the expected behavior: Godot opens in desktop 2.
* Going back to desktop 1 and creating a Finder window, and later moving to desktop 2 to launch Godot shows the problematic behavior: desktop is automatically switched to 1 and Godot's project manager launches behind the Finder window.
* After closing the FInder window in desktop 1 and going back to desktop 2, Godot launches in 2 again as expected.

summary: No engine property to adjust scrollbar gap without altering grabber border radius

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: Godot v4.5.beta5, Godot v4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta5.mono - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7602) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12700H (20 threads) - 15.62 GiB memory

### Issue description

As discussed in https://github.com/passivestar/godot-minimal-theme/issues/42, it’s very unlikely that the way borders currently work will change, since doing so could break existing projects. However, the current implementation still poses a theming limitation worth documenting.

In Godot’s engine, the vertical/horizontal scrollbar gap (space between the grabber and the container edge) is defined by transparent pixels in the default scrollbar textures.

When creating custom themes that remove the scroll area texture and display only the grabber (as many modern apps do), there’s no direct way to increase

Specifically:
1. Increasing the margin by adding a large transparent border to the grabber reduces its border radius, making it appear less rounded
2. There is no separate engine property to control the scrollbar gap independently of the grabber’s rounded corners.

This issue can be fixes if we Introduce an engine‑level margin or padding property for scrollbars that adjusts the grabber gap independently from its border radius.

### Steps to reproduce

Steps to Reproduce:
1. In a custom theme, remove the scrollbar’s scroll area texture so that only the grabber is visible.
2. Replace the grabber texture with a StyleBox (no transparent pixels) to emulate a modern “grabber‑only” design.
3. Attempt to increase the gap between the grabber and the edge of the scrollbar track by:   
a. Adding transparent borders to the grabber, or   
b. Adjusting available margin settings in the theme.
4. Observe how increasing the transparent border affects the grabber’s border radius.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

In the editor, the CollisionShape2D triggers RayCast2Ds in its original position, but is ignored by those positioned some distance away from its original position relative to the parent StaticBody2D when only it is moved there, without the parent.

Setup RayCast2Ds in parallel and in a tool script, force them to update in process so they display color changes when intersected.

summary: OS.execute_with_pipe causes SIGPIPE crash on writing to a restarted process

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.beta.custom_build.45509c284

### System information

Ubuntu - 4.5.beta.custom_build.45509c284 - x86_64

### Issue description

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2feb6d76-0739-45e2-8fc8-e35deb89b65f

Upon restarting a process, a call to pipe() in the OS_Unix driver provides `0` as the read file descriptor to `pipe_in`:
 ```cpp
// OS_Unix::execute_with_pipe, lines 737-741
int pipe_in[2] = { -1, -1 };
int pipe_out[2] = { -1, -1 };
int pipe_err[2] = { -1, -1 };

ERR_FAIL_COND_V(pipe(pipe_in) != 0, ret); // upon restart pipe_in[0] = 0

// ...
// lines 772-773,  inside forked process
::close(STDIN_FILENO);
::dup2(pipe_in[0], STDIN_FILENO); //  bad file descriptor error.

```

The editor crashes on any write operation with the bad pipe, in reference to the MRP's main.gd
```gdscript
func _on_write_pressed() -> void:
	pipe_process.stdio.store_line(%TextEdit.text) # <--- SIGPIPE happens here, applies to any write method
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run project
2. Enter text in text edit and press the button to write to stdin
3. Restart the process with either the regular restart button or restart with time button
4. Try to write to stdin again, the game will crash

- I know I saw this in other 4.5 betas, but I'm not sure which ones.  At least beta4.
- Does not happen in 4.4.1

### System information

MacOS 15.6 (24G84)

### Issue description

If you create a simple standalone script that quits, the Godot icon remains in the MacOS dock until you click on it.  It then goes away.  The job appears to be gone, I cannot find it in the activity monitor or anywhere else.  It appears to just be an issue with the dock.

```gdscript
extends SceneTree

func _init() -> void:
	print("Hello World")
	quit()
```

Awaiting for some amount of time seems to make it go away correctly.  For me, waiting .1 seconds was not enough, but .5 was.

```gdscript
extends SceneTree

func _init() -> void:
	print("Hello World")
	await create_timer(.5).timeout
	quit()
```

### Steps to reproduce

Run this, check your dock for another Godot icon.  Click the icon, it goes away.
```gdscript
extends SceneTree

func _init() -> void:
	print("Hello World")
	quit()
```


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

See steps to reproduce.

summary: Fix performance issues when moving the mouse with high polling rate on Windows

description.text:
> [!NOTE]
> 
> **Testing needed on various hardware configurations:** V-Sync on/off, various refresh rates, pen tablet.
Make sure to build with optimizations enabled, or use the Windows release export template linked below.

This leads to a significant improvement on my end, but it's still not perfectly smooth on the test projects. I've seen many other games have no problems keeping 1,000+ FPS with a 8,000 Hz mouse during fast movement[^1], so surely we have some room of improvement (unless we're bound by scripting performance).

[^1]: For example, in ezQuake, I can get 3,500 FPS when looking at a wall, and about 3,000 FPS when moving the mouse fast.

**Testing projects:**

* [InputRepro2.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22754573/InputRepro2.zip)
 (general framerate stability testing)
* [graphics_tablet_input.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21798498/graphics_tablet_input.zip) (to test pen tablets, but also drawing precision with a mouse)
* [physics_interpolation.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21798491/physics_interpolation.zip) (to test captured mouse mode FPS look)

Download this export template, extract the ZIP, move the executable to each project's folder and double-click it to run the project: [godot.windows.template_release.x86_64.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21798467/godot.windows.template_release.x86_64.zip)

* This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/60646.

Thanks to @bruvzg for helping out on this PR :slightly_smiling_face:

## TODO

* [ ] I noticed an issue with captured mouse mode that wasn't here before. In the InputRepro2 testing project, switch to captured mouse mode and click while moving the mouse. You will see the mouse cursor appear to move for one frame, even though it shouldn't as we're in captured mouse mode. This issue doesn't occur on `master`.
* [ ] Fix the issue mentioned in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/109639#issuecomment-3378842876 (I'm not sure how). It's possibly related to the item above.
* [ ] See if different values of `MAX_EVENTS_PER_FRAME` would be able to do a better job at reducing frame drops.
  * We could attempt lower values once https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/109639#issuecomment-3378842876 is fixed, but at the same time, it would also reduce precision when input accumulation is disabled (which drawing apps may require). From my testing, a value of `1` provides great frametime stability, but it also breaks the point of disabling input accumulation. Maybe it should be a project setting?
* [x] See if a different method of capping the number of events per frame (e.g. using `Engine::get_singleton()->get_process_frames()`) could be better than the current approach, which purely relies on the function scope.
  * Probably not needed for now.


## Preview

<details open>

<summary>PC specifications</summary>

- **CPU:** AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D
- **GPU:** NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090
- **RAM:** 64 GB (2×32 GB DDR5-6000 CL30)
- **SSD:** Solidigm P44 Pro 2 TB
- **OS:** Windows 11 24HZ
- **Monitor:** ASUS ROG PG32UCDP (480 Hz mode)
- **Mouse:** ASUS ROG Harpe Ace Extreme (2.4 GHz wireless, 8,000 Hz polling rate)
</details>

*Look not only at the FPS counter, but also at the mspf (frametime counter) next to it. We want it to be as stable as possible (as close as 2.1 mspf for a 480 Hz display).*

*The issue in `master` tends to be worse at lower FPS caps/refresh rates, but I wanted to test this PR in extreme scenarios to make sure as few frame drops as possible occur at very high refresh rates.*

summary: Application focus events are never emitted for embedded game windows on macOS

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.beta5

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta5 - macOS Sequoia (15.5.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 Pro (Apple8) - Apple M2 Pro (10 threads) - 32.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

The game window embedding on macOS seems to act a bit differently from how Windows and Linux/X11 works in terms of the application focus events (`NOTIFICATION_APPLICATION_FOCUS_IN` and `NOTIFICATION_APPLICATION_FOCUS_OUT`) as they're seemingly never emitted. Only if you disable the "Embed Game on Next Play" are they emitted when switching to another application.

On Windows and Linux/X11 they're even emitted when you just click outside of the actual game window part of the editor, without switching to another application or anything like that.

I believe the same thing applies to `NOTIFICATION_WM_WINDOW_FOCUS_IN` and `NOTIFICATION_WM_WINDOW_FOCUS_OUT` as well.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP on macOS
2. Ensure you have "Embed Game on Next Play" enabled
3. Ensure you have "Make Game Workspace Floating on Next Play" disabled
4. Run the MRP
5. Click outside of the game viewport
6. Click inside of the game viewport
7. Note how nothing is printed in the Output panel
8. Open the MRP on Windows or Linux (X11)
9. Do the same thing
10. Note how the events are in fact printed to the Output panel

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[application_focus_event_2025-08-15_12-00-45.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21792690/application_focus_event_2025-08-15_12-00-45.zip)

summary: LightmapGI generating light artifacts along edges of meshes

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible on Godot v4.4.1.stable - Not reproducible on Godot v4.3.stable ### System information Windows 11 - Godot v4.4.1.stable - Compatibility, Mobile, Forward+, dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 ### Issue description When baking lightmaps with the LightmapGI node, strange light artifacts appear around the edges of meshes. These light artifacts are part of the generated lightmap. I have tried numerous possible fixes and solutions, to no avail. Possible fixes I tried: - Tweaking lightmap bias - Changing number of bounces (changing to 0 removed the artifacts) - Tweaking denoiser settings - Turning off the denoiser - Changing the compression mode on the generated .exr texture - Using a custom UV2 layout with custom padding following directions per the official documentation - Making the walls on thicker on the level I believe that this is not light leaking. In the image above, there is only one light in the scene. Also in the image above, I have used a custom .glb model exported from Blender 4.0.2. The text below is what is printed in the console when baking on Compatibility mode, although this issue is present on all rendering devices. ``` ERROR: drivers/gles3/storage/texture_storage.cpp:1792 - Parameter "texture" is null. Done baking lightmaps in 00:00:01. ``` ### Steps to reproduce 1) Create a custom .glb model, and drop it into a scene (creating a new inherited scene also works) 2) Add OmniLight3D configured to be ready for lightmap baking 3) Add LightmapGI with default settings 4) Bake the LightmapGI ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21782120/project.zip) This MRP comes with the .glb used in the screenshot above, as well as a custom brick texture and its normal map. A basic player controller is provided to walk around the scene in-game.
